title: Capurodendron rubrocostatum
taxonomy: Capurodendron rubrocostatum belongs to the family Sapotaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as sapotes. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Capurodendron. It is native to South America, specifically in the countries of French Guiana and Brazil. The classification of Capurodendron rubrocostatum is further categorized under the class Magnoliopsida, order Ericales, and family Sapotaceae. The plant's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Capurodendron rubrocostatum, also known as the red-ribbed capurodendron, is a plant species found in the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Capurodendron rubrocostatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tea and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
